purpose: Tell Hermes how to interpret Report.json when the file watcher fires a Telegram alert.

watch_targets.description: Live project status, folder map, changes, and alerts
This file — interpretation rules for the agent

workflow: 4. Check status.health, status.blockers, and alerts array
5. Send a concise Telegram summary to the user

sections: What changed — bullet list from changes.since_last_report
Next — top item from status.next_steps if relevant
telegram_format.example:
📋 Project update

Scaffold complete — agentic app structure is ready.

Changes:
• Added orchestrator, tools, memory modules
• Config and SOUL.md in place

Status: ok — no blockers.

field_reference.Report.summary: Human-readable headline — use as opening line
field_reference.Report.changes.since_last_report: List of changes since last update — main body
field_reference.Report.changes.change_type: Category: initial_scaffold | feature | fix | deploy | alert
field_reference.Report.status.blockers: Issues preventing progress — mention if non-empty
field_reference.Report.alerts: Urgent items — always mention if non-empty
field_reference.Report.folder_structure: Reference only — do not dump full tree unless user asks
field_reference.Report.components: Module status map — mention if a component status changed

when_you_change_the_project: Update summary to reflect current state
Run: python scripts/sync_report.py (optional — refreshes folder tree)
update_protocol.watcher_will: Detect Report.json save → cron runs → Hermes reads both files → Telegram summary

agent_mode.prompt_hint: Report.json was updated. Follow instruction.json. Read both files from project_root and send the user a Telegram-friendly summary of what changed.
